description: Curated page-owned refinements that override generic or degraded source fields without changing the frozen curriculum objective.

learning.student_instruction: Say, ‘My name is ___.’ Trace your name once. Then write it once.
learning.expected_response: Child says own name, traces the adult-written model, and makes one supported copy attempt.

teacher.model: Write the child’s name on the trace guide, model the sentence once, and guide the first tracing movement without completing the copy line.
teacher.question: What is your name?

guidance.parent_extension: Say family members’ names and clap the syllables in each name. Let the child trace one familiar name with a finger.

illustration.scene: One child looking into a mirror while the official Star asset holds a blank name card. Keep the lower writing area completely clear.
illustration.focal_point: The child and mirror are the visual model; one dotted trace guide and one blank copy guide are built separately by the page composer.
protected_response_zones: entire deterministic trace-and-copy area below the illustration

learning.student_instruction: Name red, yellow, and blue. Match each colour, then create your own colour picture.
learning.expected_response: Child identifies the three primary colours, matches them correctly, and uses them in a simple artwork.

teacher.model: Show red, yellow, and blue colour cards. Match one card to one familiar object, then demonstrate a small colour sample without completing the child’s artwork.
teacher.question: Which primary colour will you use, and why?

guidance.parent_extension: Find one red, one yellow, and one blue object at home. Name the colours and make a small picture using safe crayons.

illustration.scene: Three large paint pots—red, yellow, and blue—beside three familiar preschool objects that clearly match those colours. Include one small finished colour sample as the model.
illustration.focal_point: The three primary colours are unmistakable, while the large child artwork area remains empty and unobstructed.
protected_response_zones: large deterministic creative-response area below the illustration
